one_line_takeaway: SCAR performs retain-set-free machine unlearning by Mahalanobis-shifting forget features to the nearest wrong-class distribution while distilling the original model through OOD images, matching retain-free baselines and approaching retain-dependent SOTA — and a self-forget variant even works from just the class ID.
key_finding: SCAR matches or beats retain-free baselines and comes close to retain-dependent SOTA despite never touching the retain set. In CR, SCAR CIFAR100 AUS = 0.935 vs retain-free Negative Gradient 0.849 and Random Labels 0.774, and within 1 std of retain-using SCRUB (0.977) / DUCK (0.931); TinyImageNet AUS 0.940 similarly tops the retain-free group. The self-forget variant (no forget AND no retain data) still reaches CIFAR100 AUS 0.929, essentially matching full SCAR. In the harder HR scenario SCAR surpasses ALL retain-free methods (CIFAR10 0.953, CIFAR100 0.934, TinyImageNet 0.886) with MIA F1 driven to ~0.50 (50.07 / 49.77 / 49.85), i.e. forget samples become statistically indistinguishable from unseen data. The distillation-trick's OOD component is essential and semantics-dependent: any real-image surrogate works (ImageNet subset best, CIFAR100 AUS 0.935; COCO 0.933) but semantics-free Gaussian noise collapses to AUS 0.388. Mahalanobis beats cosine (slightly, and ~22% faster than cosine) and L2 distance; both loss terms are needed (either alone fails). Method generalizes across CNNs and a ViT (ViT-B16 unlearned AUS 0.945).
core_contribution: The paper introduces SCAR (Selective-distillation for Class and Architecture-agnostic unleaRning), an approximate machine-unlearning method that removes targeted information from a trained image classifier WITHOUT using a retain set (the residual training data normally required to restore accuracy after unlearning). SCAR has two synergistic mechanisms authored by this paper: (1) a metric-learning forget loss that shifts each forget sample's backbone feature vector toward the nearest wrong-class feature DISTRIBUTION using a modified Mahalanobis distance (with correlation-matrix normalization, covariance shrinkage for full rank, and Tukey feature normalization) rather than toward a single centroid or logit as in prior work; and (2) a 'distillation-trick' that preserves test accuracy by distilling the frozen original model into the unlearning model via Jensen-Shannon divergence on out-of-distribution / surrogate images (e.g. an ImageNet subset), exploiting the fact that DNNs produce structured, clustered activations even on OOD inputs. The paper also contributes a 'self-forget' variant for class removal that needs only the class ID to be removed — no access to the forget set nor the retain set — by having the original model pseudo-label the surrogate dataset into surrogate-forget and surrogate-retain splits. Contribution is clearly separated from cited work: the closest cited methods DUCK [9] and Boundary Unlearning [4] shift toward nearest centroid/logit and (partially) depend on the retain set; SCAR's novelty is distribution-based Mahalanobis shifting plus fully retain-free OOD distillation.
threat_model: This is a privacy / right-to-be-forgotten setting, not an adversarial-attack-defense paper. Motivation (paper's own Introduction) is compliance with data-removal regulations (GDPR, CCPA) and mitigating privacy leakage vectors such as membership inference and model inversion attacks. The operative constraint SCAR targets is a realistic access restriction: after an unlearning/removal request, the retain set (bulk of training data) may be unavailable for legal/privacy or scale reasons, and in the hardest case even the forget-set images are unavailable (only the class ID is known). SCAR is the privacy mechanism itself; the paper does not defend against a specific active adversary. Membership inference attack (MIA) appears only as an EVALUATION metric in the Homogeneous-Removal scenario, where the desired outcome is MIA F1 = 0.5 (chance level, i.e. forget samples indistinguishable from unseen test data). No new attack is proposed, and no certified/provable unlearning guarantee is claimed (the authors flag certifiability as future work).
limitations: Authors' own stated limitation: a marginal accuracy drop versus the original model, a direct consequence of never accessing forget or retain data (e.g. CIFAR100 CR retain-test accuracy falls from ~77.5 original to 72.93). They also concede no certifiability/provable-removal guarantee — future work should mathematically explore certifiability. Reviewer-observed limitations: the self-forget variant works only in Class-Removal, not HR (the forget set is needed to identify which instances to forget), and its quality depends on surrogate size (AUS plateaus only once >=6K surrogate images are available). SCAR is slower than retain-free baselines (109.11s vs Negative Gradient 9.02s on CIFAR100 CR) and requires storing per-class covariance statistics plus a surrogate dataset (30.76 Mb total), though far less than retain-based methods (146.48 Mb) and faster than Fine-Tuning (366.99s) / SCRUB (139.18s). Evaluation is limited to image classification on three small/medium datasets; no large-scale (ImageNet-21K) results despite that being cited as motivation.

datasets_used: Surrogate/OOD datasets for distillation-trick: 10K-image ImageNet1K subset, 10K-image COCO subset, 2K natural images, 10K distilled ImageNet images, and Gaussian-noise images
quantitative_results: CR CIFAR100: SCAR At_r=72.93, At_f=2.00, AUS=0.935; SCAR Self-Forget AUS=0.929
CR CIFAR10: SCAR At_r=87.71, At_f=0.97, AUS=0.981
CR TinyImageNet: SCAR At_r=62.99, At_f=0.60, AUS=0.940
CR retain-free baselines CIFAR100 AUS: Negative Gradient 0.849, Random Labels 0.774 (SCAR 0.935 beats both)
CR retain-using SOTA CIFAR100 AUS: SCRUB 0.977, DUCK 0.931 (SCAR within 1 std)
HR AUS: SCAR CIFAR10 0.953, CIFAR100 0.934, TinyImageNet 0.886; MIA F1 ~0.50 (50.07 / 49.77 / 49.85, chance level = indistinguishable)
HR retain-free baselines much lower: Negative Gradient 0.841/0.718/0.770, Random Labels 0.807/0.711/0.760
Surrogate-dataset ablation CIFAR100 CR AUS: ImageNet subset 0.935 (best), COCO 0.933, distilled 0.923, random images 0.922, Gaussian noise 0.388 (collapse); KS test p<1e-3 confirms no data leakage
Distance ablation CIFAR100: Mahalanobis CR AUS 0.935 > Cosine 0.933 > L2 0.924; cosine ~22+/-5% slower than Mahalanobis
Architecture generalization CIFAR100 CR unlearned AUS: AllCNN 0.926, resnet18 0.935, resnet34 0.937, resnet50 0.942, ViT-B16 0.945

Compute/storage CIFAR100 CR: SCAR 109.11s / 30.76 Mb stored; Self-Forget 101.61s / 29.30 Mb; vs Fine-Tuning 366.99s, SCRUB 139.18s, DUCK 12.56s, Negative Gradient 9.02s; retain-based methods store 146.48 Mb
Distillation-trick sanity check: resnet18 trained purely on ImageNet1K surrogate keeps CIFAR100 test accuracy ~76% constant after epoch 5
